name: K 03375 (BM)
title: The Flood Tablet
description: The most famous cuneiform tablet in existence. Contains the flood narrative from Tablet XI of the Epic of Gilgamesh — the story of Utnapishtim, the ark, and the great deluge. Discovered by George Smith in 1872, who recognized its parallels to the biblical flood account.
raw_atf:
&K3375 = K 03375 (Epic of Gilgamesh, Tablet XI — Flood Tablet)
#atf: lang akk
#note: Neo-Assyrian copy, Library of Ashurbanipal, 7th c. BCE. British Museum. Excerpt: Utnapishtim's account of the flood.
@tablet
@obverse
1. Ut-na-pisz-tim a-na szu-a-szu iz-zak-kar
2. lu-uq-bi-ik-ka ni-is-ir-ti DINGIR.MES
3. i-gar-ri i-gar-ri ku-us-su-pu uz-ni-ka
4. Szu-ru-up-pak uru a-sza2-re-du
5. sza ina ki-szad pu-rat-ti szak-nu
6. uru szu-a-tu la-bi-ri-ma DINGIR.MES lib-bi-szu-nu usz-bu
7. ir-te-'-u DINGIR.MES ra-bu-ti li-ih-li-iq a-bu-bu
8. it-ti-su-nu A-num ra-bi-um
9. Be-el sa pu-uh-ri-su-nu
10. ka-szid ADAD
11. i-gar-ri i-gar-ri ku-us-su-pu uz-ni-ka
12. ma-tum li-ih-li-iq
13. e-li-ip-pa e-pu-us bu-ul-li-it na-pisz-ti-ka
14. e-pi-isz-ma e-li-ip-pa szu-a-ti usz-ra-a-ti-sza
15. la mu-da-a mi-sil szu-s,i-ka
@reverse
16. im-ta-luu sza-mu-u ri-hi-it-sa
17. it-ti sa-a-ri ta-ha-zu-ma at-bu-u-uk
18. a-di same-e u3 ers,etim uq-ta-ab-bi-ir
19. a-na ki-ma ta-ha-za-ri-i nu-usz-bu
20. la i-ta-mar a-hu-u a-ha-a-szu
21. la it-ta-di-ru DINGIR.MES a-na same-e
22. ub-lu-ma i-na s,e-rim sza-ra-pi
23. id-dam-qu-u i-na same-e ra-pa-aszi
24. u4-mu 7-kam i-nu-ma at-ta-ra-ar su-um-ma-a-tu
25. u-ma-asz-szer-szi-ma i-ta-lak-ma
26. id-da-an-na-am u-tu-er-ra-am
27. u4-mu 7-kam i-nu-ma at-ta-ra-ar sin-nin-nu
28. u-ma-asz-szer-szi-ma i-ta-lak-ma
29. la i-ta-mar ma-na-ha-tum
30. it-tu-ra-am-ma
31. u4-mu 7-kam i-nu-ma at-ta-ra-ar a-ri-ba
32. u-ma-asz-szer-szi a-ri-bi it-ta-lak-ma
33. i-mur na-ba-szu-u a-kal i-kul
34. ib-ba-al-ki-it la it-tu-ra-am-ma
$ rest broken

sections.content: &K3375 = K 03375 (Epic of Gilgamesh, Tablet XI — Flood Tablet)
#note: Neo-Assyrian copy, Library of Ashurbanipal, 7th c. BCE. British Museum. Excerpt: Utnapishtim's account of the flood.

sections.translation: Utnapishtim spoke to him:
"I will reveal to you a hidden matter,
a secret of the gods I will tell you:
Shuruppak — a city you know well,
which lies on the bank of the Euphrates —

sections.content: uru szu-a-tu la-bi-ri-ma DINGIR.MES lib-bi-szu-nu usz-bu
sections.translation: that city was ancient, and the gods within it
resolved to send a flood, the great gods all:
Anu, their father,
Bel, their counsellor,
Kashshu, the enforcer,
a secret of the gods I will tell you:
the land shall be destroyed by flood.
Build a boat; save your life.
Build that boat with its dimensions,
so that none but you may know its breadth.
The whole sky turned black with storm.
Together with the wind the battle began,
sweeping over sky and earth alike.
Like a battle charge it swept the land;
no man could see his fellow,
the gods themselves cowered in heaven,
crouching like dogs at the outer wall.
On the seventh day I released a dove.
The dove flew off but then returned —
it found no resting place and came back.
On the seventh day I released a swallow.
The swallow flew off but then returned —
it found no resting place and came back.
On the seventh day I released a raven.
The raven flew off; it saw that the waters had receded,
it ate, it preened, it did not return.
$ rest broken
